Why are you interested in this role?: What excites me about implementation is that it's where strategy meets reality—a product only creates value if people actually adopt it, and driving that adoption is exactly the work I love. At InGenius I drive activation projects for 20+ programs from kickoff to steady state, standardize the methodologies that make them repeatable, and track the KPIs that prove value stuck. Doing that in healthcare, where adoption directly affects clinicians and patients, feels genuinely meaningful—and Commure's scale and mission make the stakes real.
Tell us about your implementation / chan: Change management is my craft. At Vestas I led an Inclusive Leadership initiative across 22 nationalities and scaled it to 23 global locations using the ADKAR framework—building awareness, recruiting an internal champion, delivering the workshop, and reinforcing it with follow-ups and 1:1s. At InGenius I run end-to-end activations: defining implementation methodologies and SOPs, designing and delivering training for diverse user groups, and tracking adoption metrics. My consulting work at ODN is implementation by nature—I walk into orgs with open questions and leave with adopted frameworks. I diagnose before designing, build in feedback loops, and measure behavior, not just go-live.
Where are you less experienced, and how: I'll be candid—I haven't built operational dashboards in Retool or written production SQL, so the technical-tooling side of this role would be a genuine ramp for me. What I'd bring on day one is the harder-to-teach part: driving adoption through people and process, standardizing implementation methodology, and managing change at scale. I'm strong with data and metrics in Excel and Sheets, I learn tools quickly, and I'd invest early in understanding the technical stack deeply rather than faking fluency. I'd rather spend extra time getting the foundation right than move fast without it.
Tell us about working with healthcare /: At NextGen Healthcare I drove adoption and change across cross-functional projects with clinical and administrative stakeholders, so I understand how cautious and high-stakes healthcare environments are—and how much trust matters before anything gets adopted. My instinct is to over-invest in stakeholder relationships early (1:1 calls, listening before prescribing), which has consistently helped adoption stick. I'd bring that same patience and rigor to owning C-level relationships through the activation phase.
Why Commure?: I'm drawn to Commure because you're attacking one of healthcare's biggest problems—the trillion-dollar administrative burden—and doing it at real scale with AI integrated across 60+ EHRs. I also love that this role sits close to the work: directly alongside clinicians, with immediate impact. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat implementation as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to help your enterprise clients reach value quickly and durably.
